threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You call a 'baguette' a 'French stick' and look puzzled when someone mentions the 73 bus doesn't run after 11.30pm on weekends.
Caught a ride on the 59 last night; a guy was singing to the moon like it was his best mate. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was really living in Neukölln when I saw a guy selling handmade döner wraps from a cargo bike outside the Kottbusser Tor U-Bahn station at midnight.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find döner shops open at 4 AM, serving up fresh bread and spicy sauce to night owls coming home from clubs.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can name all the taco trucks on 16th without hesitation, but a newbie stares blankly at a menu board.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, I watched a Muni bus roll past three times, each driver waving at a guy playing guitar on the corner. His serenade never stopped.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí, but the real heart is in its small plazas, where locals gather for vermut and laughter, not selfies.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the newbies by their reluctance to embrace the 1:30 lunch hour—still holding fast to noon meal times, bless their hearts.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of the 'Underground'—the locals can spot the freshies a mile away.
Caught a girl arguing with a pigeon outside the Ritzy at 3am. She insisted it was her 'spirit animal.' Strange night in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I heard a street musician playing a remix of Berliner Luft on the U-Bahn at 3 AM while the city was half asleep.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Friedrichshain has its own 'Kiezkneipe' vibe, where you can find a pub hosting a 2 AM poetry slam, right next to a kebab stand playing 90s techno.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they know to pack an extra sweater for those freezing BART delays on a sunny day.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a saxophonist jamming at the Civic Center stop, Muni delayed yet again. Crowds just swayed and clapped, somehow made the wait feel shorter.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all tapas are created equal, but only locals know the best hidden tapas bars that serve dishes that aren't on any menu.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they stare at the metro map like it's a treasure map, lost and hopeful at the same time.
